description: A polished, advertising-style designed promo graphic encouraging undocumented immigrants to leave the U.S. voluntarily. The central image is a glossy, photorealistic (likely AI-generated or stock-style) yellow hardshell rolling suitcase with black corner guards and spinner wheels, standing on a reflective dark tiled airport floor beside a floor-to-ceiling window. Through the window, a bright yellow commercial passenger jet is parked on the tarmac in soft warm daylight, evoking imminent departure and travel. The color palette is warm gold/yellow against cool blue-gray tones. Large display typography on the right reads 'Leave' in bold gold, 'on your' and 'terms' in white, with 'OWN' emphasized in bold white, so the headline reads 'Leave on your OWN terms.' A dark navy footer bar runs along the bottom containing the DHS/ICE seal, the 'ICE' wordmark, and the text 'Learn more at: ICE.gov/self-deportation.' The messaging technique frames self-deportation as a dignified, autonomous, comfortable choice (travel/vacation aesthetic) rather than forced removal, using aspirational consumer advertising visual language. Target audience: undocumented immigrants in the U.S., aiming to pressure them toward the CBP Home / self-deportation program.
notable_text: Leave on your OWN terms. Learn more at: ICE.gov/self-deportation
